title: quercus pedunculata var. fastigiata
intro: Quercus pedunculata var. fastigiata, also known as the Fastigiata or Oak tree, is a variety of the Pedunculate Oak tree native to Europe. It is a deciduous tree with a narrow conical crown, often used in landscaping for its ornamental value. The medicinal properties of Quercus pedunculata var. fastigiata include the presence of tannins and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, and it can be consumed in various herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures.
